response.justification: Bogdan is a capable polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship habits, but his skill profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Java appears only briefly (Altom, robotic arms) and Kotlin is absent entirely; his dominant stack across the last decade is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate exposure, no JVM-specific depth, and no IntelliJ Platform or JVM IDE tooling experience. The six-month stint writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI is the closest analog to IDE internals work, but it is both dated and non-JVM. The core requirements of this role — deep JVM understanding, Spring ecosystem fluency, and enterprise Java IDE development — are simply not present.
strengths: Genuine interest in developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work, evidenced by NI LabWindows/CVI contributions and recent MCP code-generator infrastructure
Consistent software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple senior roles
Broad systems breadth — web, mobile, embedded, distributed micro-services — showing adaptability and architectural range
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience; Java appears only in a brief robotic-arm context at Altom and is not listed as a primary language
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate exposure anywhere in the resume — a non-negotiable requirement for this position
Primary language stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is entirely outside the JVM ecosystem this role is built around
